cards.front: What are IPC methods used for?
cards.back: Inter-Process Communication (IPC) methods are used to allow different processes to communicate with each other.

cards.front: What is the purpose of a pipe in IPC?
cards.back: A pipe in IPC is used to create a unidirectional communication channel between two processes.
The popen function opens a process for reading or writing and returns a file pointer to the opened process.
The pclose function closes a process opened by the popen function and returns the return value of the closed process.

cards.front: What is a FIFO in IPC?
cards.back: A FIFO (First-In-First-Out) in IPC is a special type of pipe that allows processes to communicate with each other in a sequential manner.
